data.title: PulteGroup, Toll Bros., and Masco upgraded at Evercore
data.description:
Evercore ISI became more optimistic about the homebuilding industry as builder stocks declined ~15% since analyst Stephen Kim downgraded the group.

He raised homebuilders PulteGroup (PHM) and Toll Brothers (TOL) and building materials company Masco (MAS) to Outperform from In-Line.

"We think the market has priced in much of the bad news already," Kim wrote in a note to clients. "Indeed, the homebuilders hit their historic buy signal last week when the median small-cap builder traded below 0.80x tangible book value."

The analyst selected Pulte (PHM), Toll (TOL), and Masco (MAS) for upgrades because they are "relatively more resilient than peers to the current industry challenges."

For example, PHM and TOL "have sustained meaningfully higher operating margins than the majority of their peers and have seen considerably less deterioration relative to 4Q19," Kim wrote.

The upgrade comes even as Evercore ISI increased its estimate for the 30-year fixed-rate mortgage rate to 6.25% for FY2026, up about 25 basis points from its previous expectation. Because of the challenging environment, the firm also reduced earnings estimates for homebuilders.

The SA Quant system rates PulteGroup (PHM), Toll  (TOL), and  Masco (MAS) as Holds.

In Monday premarket trading, Pulte (PHM) fell 2.3%, Toll (TOL) declined 3.0%, and Masco (MAS) rose 1.0%.

Last week, Seaport Research lowered ratings on homebuilders over intensifying concern that housing activity will slow further on a lower neutral job rate.

data.title: Elektros flags rise in used EV sales amid higher gasoline prices
data.description:
• Elektros (ELEK) on Monday said that used electric vehicle (EV) sales in the United States rose 12% year-on-year to 93,500 units in the first quarter of 2026, as demand strengthened amid rising gasoline prices and improving affordability.
  • The company said used EV prices are nearing parity with gasoline vehicles, averaging about $1,300 more, while turnover rates on dealer lots are now comparable, indicating stronger consumer acceptance.
  • New EV sales faced pressure after the expiration of a $7,500 federal tax credit in September 2025, pushing price-sensitive buyers toward the used market, which the company described as a transitional shift.
  • Elektros added that higher fuel costs and increased consumer interest are supporting EV demand, while expanding charging infrastructure with over 71,000 U.S. public fast-charging ports as of April 1 is improving accessibility.
  • ELEK closed -10.14% at $0.0062.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Monday: RVMD, GS, BKR, MCD
data.description:
Stock index futures fell on Monday after U.S.-Iran talks collapsed and Washington announced it will begin a blockade of Iranian ports.

Here are the four stocks to watch on the day:

Revolution Medicines (RVMD) surged 30.66% in premarket trading after a potential cancer drug, daraxonrasib, helped patients with advanced pancreatic cancer live longer than those on standard chemotherapy in a late-stage trial. Median overall survival was 13.2 months compared with 6.7 months, a notable improvement in a disease with few effective treatments. The company said it plans to seek regulatory approval based on the results.

Goldman Sachs (GS) slipped 3.5% before the opening bell after the Wall Street bank’s fourth-quarter net interest income fell short and its provision for credit losses came in higher than consensus estimates. The bottom line exceeded expectations, helped by strong top-line results in its Global Banking & Markets division, which offset a quarter-over-quarter dip in Wealth Management revenue.

Baker Hughes (BKR) gained 1.67% in premarket trade after the oilfield services firm announced an agreement to sell its Waygate Technologies business to Hexagon in an all-cash transaction valued at approximately $1.45 billion. The sale encompasses Waygate Technologies’ remote visual inspection, ultrasound, radiography, and imaging solutions portfolios, along with all assets of the business, including intellectual property and resources.

McDonald’s (MCD) is planning to offer new energy drinks and specialty sodas at its U.S. restaurants this year, The Wall Street Journal reported, citing people familiar with the matter and company documents. The new beverages include Red Bull Dragonberry Energizer, Dirty Dr Pepper, and Mango Pineapple Refresher. The specialty sodas are slated to launch next month, while the new energy drinks will be rolled out starting in August.

data.title: Fastenal posts in-line Q1 results as shares slip premarket
data.description:
Fastenal (FAST) reported first-quarter results that largely met Wall Street expectations, though its shares slipped 1.4% in premarket trading Monday.

Fastenal (FAST) posted revenue of $2.2 billion for the quarter ended March 31, in line with the consensus estimate and up 12.4% from a year earlier, driven by broad-based demand and customer share gains.

Daily sales also rose 12.4% year over year, reflecting strength across manufacturing and non-residential construction end markets, as well as continued momentum with key account customers.

Earnings hold steady, margins improve

Fastenal (FAST) reported earnings of $0.30 a share, matching analysts’ expectations, as net income rose to $339.8 million, or $0.30 a share, from $298.7 million, or $0.26 a share, a year earlier.

Operating income increased 13.6% to $447.6 million, with operating margin expanding modestly to 20.3% from 20.1%, supported by cost discipline and productivity gains.

Gross margin, however, slipped to 44.6% from 45.1%, reflecting pricing pressures, transportation costs and a shift toward larger customers with lower margins.

Technology, customer strategy drive growth

Fastenal (FAST) continued to benefit from its investments in digital and onsite service capabilities, including its FASTBin and FASTVend inventory management systems. Sales through these technology-enabled channels grew at a double-digit pace and accounted for nearly 45% of total revenue.

The company also reported strong growth in contract customers, which now represent more than 75% of sales, underscoring the effectiveness of its strategy to deepen relationships with larger, multi-site clients.

In the earnings release, Chief Executive Dan Florness said, “Our sales performance reflects the contribution from improved customer contract signings since the first quarter of 2024.”

Cash flow, shareholder returns

Fastenal (FAST) generated operating cash flow of $378 million in the quarter, representing 111% of net income, supported by disciplined working capital management.

The company returned $296 million to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases during the period, continuing its focus on capital returns.

Looking ahead, Fastenal (FAST) said it expects capital expenditures of between $310 million and $330 million in 2026, driven by investments in distribution infrastructure, technology and transportation capacity.

data.title: Ferguson Enterprises Inc. declares $0.89 dividend
data.description:
• Ferguson Enterprises Inc. (FERG) declares $0.89/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 1.39%

  • See FERG Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Alpha Modus converts preferred stock to boost Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• Alpha Modus (AMOD) is converting 90% of its remaining Series C preferred stock (held by the CEO’s family trusts) into common equity to simplify its capital structure.
  • The move eliminates most preferred stock overhang and aligns insider ownership with common shareholders.
  • The CEO’s family will give up all preferred protections, including liquidation preferences and price-based conversion terms.
  • Shares issued in the exchange are locked up until June 13, 2026.
  • Over $71M in convertible preferred stock will be removed from the balance sheet after this and prior actions.
  • Only ~430,000 preferred shares held by the chief strategy officer will remain outstanding.
  • Alpha Modus has 45 days to submit a compliance plan to Nasdaq and up to 180 days to regain compliance if approved.

data.title: Supreme Critical Metals to rebrand as Maverick Gold and Silver
data.description:
• Supreme Critical Metals (VRCFF) has rebranded to Maverick Gold and Silver.
  • The company's trading symbol will change to "MAV" for its common shares, expected at the opening of trading on April 15, 2026. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: T. Rowe Price sees a dip in March-end AUM
data.description:
T. Rowe Price Group (TROW) said its March-end assets under management reached $1.71 trillion, down from $1.80 trillion recorded at the end of the previous month.

Net outflows were recorded at $3.2B for March, compared to $5.3B during the prior month.

By asset class, Equity had $810B in AUM as of March 31; Fixed income, including money market, had $215B in AUM; Multi-asset had $625B; and Alternatives had $60B.

Notably, the S&P 500 Index declined by 5.09% during March to close at 6,528.47 points.

data.title: Persian Gulf blockade would backfire, drive oil prices higher, Trita Parsi says—CNBC interview
data.description:
President Donald Trump’s threats to blockade the Persian Gulf would only worsen his situation by driving up oil and gas prices, according to Trita Parsi from the Quincy Institute for Responsible Statecraft. 

In an interview with CNBC, the expert argued that Iran currently holds “escalation control” in the conflict, meaning any U.S. escalation can be countered by Iran in ways that hurt Trump even more.

“An American blockade of the Persian Gulf would only make Trump’s own situation worse,” the analyst said.

“You would have less oil on the markets just from that move alone, which would further increase oil prices and gas prices here in the U.S. and intensify the energy crisis in Asia.” Parsi noted that if the Houthis in Yemen responded by closing the Red Sea, another 12% of global oil flow would be removed from the market.

Despite escalating rhetoric and failed talks in Islamabad, the analyst emphasized that neither side has declared diplomacy over, with eight days remaining in the two-week ceasefire.

“These moves, these tactics, should be considered exercises within the current negotiations,” rather than attempts to end diplomacy altogether, he explained.

A central unresolved issue is whether Lebanon will be included in any ceasefire agreement. Parsi argued Iran will likely insist on this point, partly to demonstrate it hasn’t abandoned Hezbollah but more critically to test whether the United States can actually restrain Israel.

"If the Israelis can restart the war and the United States cannot help but get involved in that war, then why should the Iranians make a deal with the United States if the ultimate decision-making is actually conducted in Israel?” he asked.

Looking ahead, Parsi warned that prolonged high oil prices through the midterm elections could prove politically devastating for Trump, potentially costing Republicans control of Congress. 

“His entire presidency may actually politically be defeated because of this war that he unnecessarily started,” Parsi concluded, noting that only a diplomatic resolution—not maximalist demands—could salvage the situation.

data.title: Klarna partners with Germany's largest gaming retailer
data.description:
Klarna Group (KLAR) said on Monday its collaboration with Germany's largest gaming retailer will bring its flexible payment options to Mindfactory's online checkout.

The partnership underscores Klarna's (KLAR) continued expansion across key retail segments, with more than a million retailers now offering the company's financing options.

With the partnership, Mindfactory customers can pay in full, pay later, or split their purchases into interest-free installments, Klarna said.

Klarna (KLAR) stock slipped 0.5% in premarket trading.
